settings.title: Meet the People Who Clean Your Teeth
settings.excerpt: Our two hygienists between them have cleaned teeth in this town for over twenty years.
settings.body:
You probably spend more time with our hygienists than with anyone else at Brightside, so it is worth introducing them properly.

Between them, Maria and Daniel have cleaned teeth in this town for over twenty years, and most of what they do is quiet, careful work that you rarely think about until it is missing.

Maria

Maria has been with us since the practice opened and is the person to ask if you are nervous about scaling. She is gentle and explains every step before it happens, and she will stop the moment you raise a hand.

She has a particular soft spot for patients who have stayed away from the dentist for years out of worry. If that is you, she is the one to see first.

Daniel

Daniel joined us two years ago from a hospital dental unit and has a real knack for working with children and first time patients. He turns a checkup into something closer to a chat, which makes all the difference for an anxious eight year old.

A few things our hygienists wish more people knew:

  • A cleaning should not hurt, and they would rather go slowly than rush you.
  • Sensitive teeth can almost always be managed, so tell them.
  • How you brush at home matters more than the visit itself.

Next time you are in the chair, feel free to ask them anything. They would much rather answer a small question now than have you worry about it at home.
